title: Void or supersede an erroneous transaction
preconditions: The transaction exists; its provenance and current correction state are inspectable; the agent has a reason and idempotency identity for the corrective action.
postconditions: The invalid fact no longer affects active actuals; evidence and reconciliation history remain auditable; no replacement inherits financial meaning silently; exact pool/category results include only valid active state.
main_flow: 1. The caller retrieves the transaction, evidence links, reconciliation history, category and pool assignments, instrument or cardholder attribution, active transfer or refund relationships, and correction history. 2. The owner explicitly chooses void or supersession with reason and idempotency identity. 3. Tally records an attributable lifecycle event without editing original facts and atomically retires active financial relationships. 4. A replacement receives its own identity and only explicitly supplied new evidence or attribution; no relationship, evidence confirmation, category, or pool assignment moves silently. 5. Reconciliation decisions affected by the inactive transaction remain historical and expose an explicit exception or unresolved current state. 6. Active queries exclude the invalid fact and include only explicitly established replacement state. 7. History exposes the complete before and after outcome.
alt_flows: 1a. Transaction does not exist -> return not found
2a. Reason or idempotency identity is absent -> reject before mutation
3a. Same correction already committed -> return the original result
3b. Transaction was corrected incompatibly -> return conflict
3c. Relationships cannot retire or reconciliation state cannot remain coherent atomically -> apply no correction
4a. Replacement validation fails -> create neither replacement nor partial supersession
6a. History is explicitly requested -> include inactive facts and decisions but never count both outcomes as active
metadata: {"depth_tier":3,"primary_actor":"P1","trigger":"The owner discovers that a manually entered or upstream-normalized Canonical Transaction contains incorrect source facts or should not contribute to active ledger actuals.","status":"draft"}
